title: arg_session: Ensure created void* types have arch.
body:
This PR fixes the following exception:

```python
File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:878, in SimCC.arg_locs(self, prototype)
    876 if prototype._arch is None:
    877     prototype = prototype.with_arch(self.arch)
--> 878 session = self.arg_session(prototype.returnty)
    879 return [self.next_arg(session, arg_ty) for arg_ty in prototype.args]

File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:728, in SimCC.arg_session(self, ret_ty)
    726 session = self.ArgSession(self)
    727 if self.return_in_implicit_outparam(ret_ty):
--> 728     self.next_arg(session, SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()))
    729 return session

File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:1614, in SimCCSystemVAMD64.next_arg(self, session, arg_type)
   1612     arg_type = SimTypePointer(arg_type.elem_type).with_arch(self.arch)
   1613 state = session.getstate()
-> 1614 classification = self._classify(arg_type)
   1615 try:
   1616     mapped_classes = []

File F:\angr\angr\angr\calling_conventions.py:1678, in SimCCSystemVAMD64._classify(self, ty, chunksize)
   1676     chunksize = self.arch.bytes
   1677 # treat BOT as INTEGER
-> 1678 nchunks = 1 if isinstance(ty, SimTypeBottom) else (ty.size // self.arch.byte_width + chunksize - 1) // chunksize
   1679 if isinstance(ty, (SimTypeFloat,)):
   1680     return ["SSE"] + ["SSEUP"] * (nchunks - 1)

File F:\angr\angr\angr\sim_type.py:778, in SimTypePointer.size(self)
    775 @property
    776 def size(self):
    777     if self._arch is None:
--> 778         raise ValueError("Can't tell my size without an arch!")
    779     return self._arch.bits

ValueError: Can't tell my size without an arch!
```

step_plan.summary: Attach the calling-convention's architecture to ephemeral void* pointer types created for implicit outparam handling in SimCC.arg_session and SimCC.return_val by calling .with_arch(self.arch) on SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) prior to passing them to next_arg/classification logic, preventing Size/arch-related ValueErrors.

steps.operation: Bind arch to ephemeral void* before passing to next_arg in arg_session
steps.reason: arg_session currently constructs a temporary SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) and calls next_arg(session, <that pointer>) when return_in_implicit_outparam(ret_ty) is True. Classification code (e.g., SystemV _classify) calls .size on the pointer and expects an architecture to be attached. Attaching the CC's arch via .with_arch(self.arch) ensures SimTypePointer.size can compute a valid size and that the pointed-to type is arch-aware.
steps.side_effects: Fixes the ValueError by ensuring size queries succeed. Tests: calling-convention tests (especially System V AMD64), any unit tests that exercise arg_locs/arg_session and implicit outparam handling. Dependents: functions that rely on next_arg/_classify will now always receive arch-bound types; code that intentionally created unbound pointers (rare) will see them bound earlier. API: no public API changes; internal type objects get an _arch set earlier which may alter any logic expecting unbound types.

steps.operation: Bind arch to ephemeral void* before passing to next_arg in return_val implicit-outparam branch
steps.reason: In the branch where an implicit outparam is requested and perspective_returned is False, return_val creates a temporary SimTypePointer(SimTypeBottom()) and passes it to next_arg without binding an arch. Classification and size computation require the pointer to have an _arch. Calling .with_arch(self.arch) on the pointer before passing it to next_arg ensures correct classification and prevents ValueError from SimTypePointer.size.
steps.side_effects: Fixes crashes when return_val triggers implicit outparam handling. Tests: run calling-convention and return-value tests across ABIs (especially System V AMD64) and any high-level tests that trigger return-by-reference. Dependents: behavior of return_val remains functionally identical except the temporary pointer is arch-bound earlier; code that expected an unbound temporary pointer may be affected. API: no external API changes, only internal type-object arch propagation is altered.
